noc_docs/development/documentation-polic.title: Documentation Policy
noc_docs/development/documentation-polic.summary: Update NOC docs only when the change affects: - behavior - requirements - API or CLI contracts - configuration - tests or verification strategy - deployment or operations - guardrails - architecture or module boundaries Pure formatting, internal cleanup, or no-behavior-change refactors do not require feature doc update

noc_docs/development/git-workflow.md.title: Git Workflow
noc_docs/development/git-workflow.md.summary: - Use `feature/<short-name>` for features. - Use `fix/<short-name>` for fixes. - Use `docs/<short-name>` for documentation-only changes. Use Conventional Commits: - `feat: add password reset` - `fix: handle expired sessions` - `docs: update login status`

noc_docs/development/testing.md.title: Testing
noc_docs/development/testing.md.summary: - Unit tests: `python -m unittest discover -s tests` - Protocol validation: `python scripts/noc.py validate` - Release check: `python scripts/release.py --check` - Compile check: `python -m py_compile scripts/__init__.py scripts/noc.py scripts/init-noc-docs.py scripts/index-noc-docs.py scripts/release.py scripts/valida

noc_docs/docs-map.md.title: Docs Map
noc_docs/docs-map.md.summary: New projects use v2 simplified by default. Run `noc work <project> --path <code/path> --json` and read the routed project memory: - `noc_docs/project.md` - `noc_docs/guardrails.md` - `noc_docs/verification.md` After a change, classify the actual diff by memory impact: `none`, `project`, `guardrails`, or `verification`.

noc_docs/features/agent-protocol/change-.title: Change Record: agent-protocol
noc_docs/features/agent-protocol/change-.summary: - Replaced the old mode-based reading policy in current docs maps, Skill workflow references, templates, examples, and token policy. - Standardized current guidance on default v2 simplified routing, three project memory files, and semantic memory impact.

noc_docs/features/agent-protocol/notes.m.title: Notes: agent-protocol
noc_docs/features/agent-protocol/notes.m.summary: - How much Claude/Gemini-specific guidance should live in their templates versus the canonical AGENTS block? - Template consistency is currently checked by targeted text assertions, not a full protocol diff. - Add examples showing a generic agent consuming `work --json`.

noc_docs/features/cli-core/change-record.title: Change Record: cli-core
noc_docs/features/cli-core/change-record.summary: - Added repeatable `--memory-impact` categories and machine-readable check output. - Made `none` pass without documentation changes and durable impacts require only their corresponding project memory. - Kept semantic judgment in the Skill; the CLI only verifies the declared impact against the Git diff.

noc_docs/features/cli-core/notes.md.title: Notes: cli-core
noc_docs/features/cli-core/notes.md.summary: - Should `doctor` and `check` also grow `--json` in the next iteration? - `scripts/noc.py` is a large single-file CLI. Keep changes localized unless a split becomes necessary for real complexity. - Add stable schema documentation for `work --json`.

noc_docs/features/codex-skill/change-rec.title: Change Record: codex-skill
noc_docs/features/codex-skill/change-rec.summary: - Split Definition of Done into v2 simplified and v1 legacy sections in both maintained Skill trees. - Kept the runtime content synchronized and added a regression assertion that v2 does not require v1 document types. - Removed obsolete workflow-mode guidance from both synchronized runtime references.

noc_docs/features/codex-skill/notes.md.title: Notes: codex-skill
noc_docs/features/codex-skill/notes.md.summary: - Should Codex eval prompts cover JSON output usage explicitly? - Skill evals check trigger examples, but do not execute a full coding workflow. - Add a short Codex prompt recipe for using `work --json` before implementation.

noc_docs/features/index.md.title: Features
noc_docs/features/index.md.summary: | Feature | Status | Entry | Requirements | Current Status | Guardrails | Tests | |---|---|---|---|---|---|---| | agent-protocol | active | ./agent-protocol/agent-guide.md | ./agent-protocol/requirements.md | ./agent-protocol/status.md | ./agent-protocol/guardrails.md | ./agent-protocol/test-record.md |

noc_docs/features/init-index-validate/ch.title: Change Record: init-index-validate
noc_docs/features/init-index-validate/ch.summary: - Made bare `noc init <project>` create only project, guardrails, verification, routing, config, manifest, and the managed AGENTS block. - Added safe project-file detection, idempotent writes, internal indexing, and v2 validation/doctor support.

noc_docs/features/init-index-validate/no.title: Notes: init-index-validate
noc_docs/features/init-index-validate/no.summary: - Should `suggest-map` explain empty suggestions more explicitly for flat CLI/tooling repositories? - Auto mode heuristics are intentionally simple and covered by examples rather than full static analysis. - Add `doctor --json` after `work --json` proves useful.

noc_docs/features/release-quality/change.title: Change Record: release-quality
noc_docs/features/release-quality/change.summary: - Used the tested release CLI patch bump to create the 1.2.1 changelog entry and update `VERSION`. - Aligned package metadata, bilingual README markers, the canonical Skill manifest, current-version assertions, and current living-doc status at 1.2.1.

noc_docs/features/release-quality/notes..title: Notes: release-quality
noc_docs/features/release-quality/notes..summary: - Should release checks include a direct `noc doctor .` gate now that the repo dogfoods NOC? - Tests use subprocess calls heavily, which is faithful but slower than unit-level parser tests. - Add CI coverage for `python scripts/noc.py work . --path scripts/noc.py --json`.
